title: Abad Sayyarah wa Chotidar Tara
gloss: A small book giving an account of the earth, the solar system and other phenomena, such as meteors, the milky-way, comets, &c., with their formation, size, distance from the earth and the sun, revolutions and the creatures inhabiting them, compiled by Lala Tara Chand, Pleader, Bikáner State.
notes: Popular astronomy (Halley's comet year) including 'creatures inhabiting' the planets; compiler a pleader in Bikaner, publishing from Bahawalpur.

author: Jiwan Singh, Munshi
title: Gulshan Pahara
gloss: Mathematical tables for use in the 2nd and 3rd Lower Primary Classes.
publisher: The Author who is teacher in the Aided School, Pul Kanjri, Amritsar district
notes: Entry preceded by '(Designed for educational purposes.)'.

author: Rang Bihari Lal, Lala
title: Talim-ul-Hisab
gloss: An arithmetic for the use of teachers of Primary Schools and pupils of Normal Schools.
publisher: The Author who is Head Master, Normal School, Ráwalpindi
copyright: Lala Rang Bihari Lal, Ráwalpindi; No. 148, dated the 17th September, 1910.
notes: Same Rang Behari Lal who translated Caleb's Eyesight in Schools (p32 s21).

author: Abdur Rahman, Hafiz
title: Siyahat-i-Hind
gloss: An account of the author's travels for seven years to the important cities throughout India.
notes: Seven-year India travelogue, 448 pp. — vernacular travel writing mapping the subcontinent.

author: Abdul Aziz, Muhammad, Maulvi
title: Aziz-ul-Akhlaq
gloss: A book on Muhammadan precepts, with quotations from the Quran and the traditions.
copyright: M. Abdur Rahman and M. Ahmad Abdullah, Deccan; No. 96, dated the 7th July, 1910.
notes: Date printed only as 7th Rajab, Hijri 1319 (≈ Oct 1901 — likely first-edition date). Same Deccan author/heirs as p45 s67 (copyright Nos. 96–97 consecutive). Hyderabad state press.

flags.issue: only Hijri 1319 printed; no Christian date given

title: Kitab-us-Salaut Sindhi
gloss: A booklet describing injunctions to be observed in performing various kinds of prayers with khutbas or sermons, read prior to performing Juma (Friday) congregational prayer.
notes: Hindu book-seller (Pokar Das of Shikarpur) publishing Muslim prayer manual — his sixth entry this quarter.

section: BILINGUAL (ARABIC AND PUNJABI IN PERSIAN CHARACTER)—POETRY.
author: Barkat Ram, Pandit
title: Anisul-Ghuraba
gloss: A booklet in which symptoms of certain diseases have been mentioned. It is said to be the translation of Hippocrates' book Qabriya in Greek which was rendered into Arabic by Hussain ibn Ishaq during the reign of Caliph Al-Mamun-ur-Rashid. The author has translated from the Arabic version.
publisher: The author, a resident of Nunar, tahsil Pasrúr, Siálkot district
copyright: P. Barkat Ram, Siálkot district; No. 113, dated the 14th July 1910.
notes: Greek→Arabic→Punjabi transmission chain: Hippocrates via Hunayn ibn Ishaq (Ma'mun-era Baghdad) translated by a village pandit — millennium-long knowledge circulation caught in the register.
marks: X at section header; pencil '91' right margin
